questions.question: The deliberate effort to avoid behaving abnormally when faced with pressure can be termed
questions.explanation: Self-control is the ability to regulate one's emotions and behaviors in the face of external pressures.

questions.question: The categories of Nigerian citizens that cannot be declared as non-citizens under any circumstance are citizens by
questions.explanation: Citizenship by birth is an inalienable right in Nigeria and cannot be revoked.

questions.question: An importance of effective parenting is that it ensures
options: adequate formal education of the children
provision of secondary needs for the family
family members know how to socialize
morally upright wards are bred for the society
questions.explanation: Effective parenting focuses on instilling values and morals that benefit the larger society.

questions.question: A ban on public lectures and symposiums is against the right to freedom of
questions.explanation: Lectures and symposiums are forms of gathering; banning them violates the right to assembly.

questions.question: All these can be a cause of accident on the road except
questions.explanation: Observing road signs is a safety measure, not a cause of accidents.

questions.question: Being truthful to oneself; being truthful to others; being someone others can trust; doing what is right regardless of the circumstances. The above quotation is apt for describing
questions.explanation: Integrity involves consistency in moral principles and honesty in all situations.

questions.question: National consciousness, unity and integrity are promoted through all the following ways except
options: compromise of the rule of law
teaching of civic education in schools
questions.explanation: Compromising the rule of law undermines national integrity and justice.

questions.question: Membership of an organization associated with secrecy of operations in the society is an indication of
questions.explanation: Cultism is defined by clandestine operations and secret rituals.

questions.question: Associations, connections and affiliations between two or more people is what type of relationship?
questions.explanation: Interpersonal relationships are the social associations between individuals.

questions.question: Orderliness in society is best guaranteed when all citizens
options: are proud to be associated with the society
know the sanctions for violation of laws
questions.explanation: Obedience to established laws is the primary foundation for social order.

questions.question: The traffic sign in the image is to caution road drivers that
options: pedestrians crossing is in progress therefore, all cars should wait patiently for them
a designated bus-stop is ahead where school children can be picked and dropped
pedestrians are to hold hands together so as to support one another when crosssing
they need to move fast because of the presence of a school zone
questions.explanation: The sign depicts a pedestrian crossing, warning drivers to yield to those on foot.

questions.question: Paying our taxes and assisting the government in the fight against crime are examples of
questions.explanation: Civil obligations are duties required by the state from its citizens.

questions.question: Creating an enabling environment for someone to have control over his financial situation is known as
questions.explanation: Empowerment provides individuals with the skills and resources to become self-reliant.

questions.question: The following are steps towards sustaining national unity except
options: emphasis on factors that unite a nation
questions.explanation: Inter-religious marriages actually promote unity; discouraging them fosters division.

questions.question: A major reason that fosters healthy interpersonal relationship is
questions.explanation: Sharing common goals brings people together and encourages cooperation.

questions.question: People living with HIV/AIDS (PLWHA) are stigmatized by
options: refusal to be voted for in election
questions.explanation: Stigmatization often results in the unfair denial of rights or privileges based on health status.

questions.question: A child who is forced to engage in hawking all day must have been denied the right to
questions.explanation: Child labor (hawking) prevents children from attending school, violating their right to education.

questions.question: The sole aim of youth empowerment is to
options: encourage students to take on a vacation job
learn to earn money and also how to manage it
learn how to behave in a work related environment
give youths financial independence and self reliance
questions.explanation: Empowerment aims to make youths independent and productive members of society.

questions.question: From the passage, it could be inferred that Chalyn is majorly a victim of
questions.explanation: The passage describes her being taken abroad by a trafficking agent for prostitution.

questions.question: Which of the following would have likely saved Chalyn from falling into the hands of traffickers?
options: moral upbringing and solid parental care
questions.explanation: Parental care and strong morals provide a defense against negative peer pressure.

questions.question: Chalyn's ordeal would have been likely prevented through all the following means except
questions.explanation: While education is important, the immediate preventative factors for a 16-year-old in that scenario were economic or social supports.

questions.question: Which of the following agents is not saddled with the responsibility of maintaining traffic regulation in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: The Customs Service handles trade and border security, not road traffic regulation.

questions.question: A society that is free from HIV/AIDS infected persons will likely experience
questions.explanation: A healthy workforce leads to higher economic productivity.

questions.question: Major behavioural manifestations of drug addicts include the following except
questions.explanation: Drug addiction typically leads to negative behaviors; humility is not a standard symptom.

questions.question: The lowest court in Nigeria is the
questions.explanation: Magistrate or District courts handle the most basic level of legal proceedings.

questions.question: Which of the following has the power to impeach the president in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Impeachment is a legislative process carried out by the National Assembly.

questions.question: The phrase 'no man is an island' means that
options: mankind needs other plants and animals to survive
citizens cannot operate without help of a government
individuals need the cooperation of others to progress
humans need to marry and have life partners
questions.explanation: This emphasizes the social and interdependent nature of humans.

questions.question: The people depicted in the image are engaged in
questions.explanation: The image likely depicts a skill acquisition session common in empowerment programs.

questions.question: The aim of those engaged in the activity captured in the image is to
questions.explanation: Vocational empowerment focus on gaining practical, relevant skills.

questions.question: Which type of skill is dominant in the image?
questions.explanation: Manipulative skills involve physical handling of tools and materials.

questions.question: Citizenship education ensures that citizens become aware of
options: tenets of the laws of the society
attributes required to become a leader
how to restore peace during conflict
questions.explanation: It aims to inform citizens about the laws, rights, and duties in their country.

questions.question: Cultism is usually not associated with
questions.explanation: Cultism disrupts studies and is rarely associated with good academic performance.

questions.question: The programmes political parties intend to implement if they are voted into power are known as political
questions.explanation: A manifesto is a published document of a party's goals and policies.

questions.question: From the dialogue, Mr. Katoun has demonstrated
questions.explanation: Refusing to vote because of lack of interest in the governor is a sign of political apathy.

questions.question: As seen in the dialogue, Mr. Ganaba is involved in an essential community service as
questions.explanation: He is encouraging his neighbor to participate in the civic process of voting.

questions.question: The behaviour of Mr. Katoun can be traced to the following factors except
questions.explanation: Technology (like BVAS) usually improves trust; it is corruption and broken promises that cause apathy.

questions.question: The constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria from 1979 to 1999 have been
questions.explanation: Nigerian constitutions are codified (written) and require a special process to amend (rigid).

questions.question: An established way Labour Unions protect the interest of workers is
questions.explanation: Industrial actions (strikes) are the primary tool for labor negotiations.

questions.question: The right to vie for public office falls under
questions.explanation: Seeking office is a fundamental political right of a citizen.

questions.question: A system of government where the ultimate power resides in the people is
questions.explanation: Democracy is defined as power belonging to the people.

questions.question: Politically enlightened people may still feel discouraged in the political affairs of their society because of political
questions.explanation: Marginalization leads to a feeling of exclusion, causing people to withdraw from politics.

questions.question: Supervisory Councillors at the local government level are part of the
questions.explanation: Councillors act as the executive cabinet for the local government area.

questions.question: Constitutional provisions cannot be derived from
questions.explanation: While colonial history exists, modern constitutional provisions are based on sovereign laws and local customs.

questions.question: Political participation is facilitated by the following reasons except
options: enhancement of the dividends of democracy
influencing the direction of government policies
questions.explanation: Political participation focuses on policy and stability rather than individual 'moral upliftment'.

questions.question: Which of these societal values is most expected of every public office holder?
questions.explanation: Public officials must be answerable to the people they serve.

questions.question: Constitutional democracy is also known as
questions.explanation: Constitutions place limits on the power of government officials.

questions.question: The phrase 'the music has changed' refers to the fact that
options: checks and balances are no longer effective
public servants are to show partisanship
there was corruption in the public service sector
the activities of the judiciary should be checkmated
questions.explanation: Political cartoons often use metaphors to highlight shifts in systemic corruption.

questions.question: The first statement in the cartoon affirms that the speaker was
questions.explanation: The context implies the character was involved in or ignored corrupt practices.

questions.question: A way Chief Jeunkoku can evade sanction is if there is
options: operational principle of rule of law
questions.explanation: Corruption thrives when the judicial system fails to hold powerful people accountable.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a feature of civil society?
options: Protection of the interest of society
questions.explanation: Civil societies are meant to be independent; seeking political patronage (favoritism) compromises them.
